commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6563de6459c62e3be5ac4e701d9609654f6a7b2d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends agent session write-lock calls so message persistence and auto-compaction can request owned transcript-write publication, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +55. Total +68 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main locks `message_end` and compaction writes, but the embedded production wrapper does not forward owned-write publication options to the fence controller. I did not run a live Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This draft needs author/maintainer follow-up for the concrete option-forwarding defect plus final live Telegram proof, so it is not a safe automated repair lane candidate.

target.title: fix(agents): publish embedded transcript writes
target.body:
Opened on behalf of Onur Solmaz (`osolmaz`). This is in progress; the fix has local regression proof and is deployed to the private HF test Space, with one post-deploy inbound Telegram confirmation still pending.

AI-assisted change.

## Summary

Telegram users could receive a full streamed answer and then still get the generic error.
The embedded session fence was treating OpenClaw-owned session-file writes as external takeovers while the prompt lock was released.
This change publishes both normal message persistence and auto-compaction appends as owned writes, so the fence advances for OpenClaw's own transcript progress while still rejecting external edits.

## What Changed

The embedded session lock already has an owned-write publication path.
Some session writes used it, but `AgentSession` message persistence and auto-compaction did not consistently publish through it.

- Extended `AgentSessionWriteLockRunner` so callers can request owned-write publication.
- Forwarded the write-lock options through `AgentSession.runWithSessionWriteLock`.
- Published `message_end` persistence with `{ publishOwnedWrite: true }`.
- Published the compaction-row append in `runCompactionWork()` with `{ publishOwnedWrite: true }`.
- Added focused coverage for compaction fence advancement and production-path coverage that `message_end` persistence requests owned-write publication.

## Testing

The focused regression tests and the core test typecheck pass on this branch.
A fixed image from the branch was also deployed to the private Hugging Face Space and reached a healthy startup state.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/sessions/sdk.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `codex review --base origin/main` before the message-end extension of the fix; no actionable correctness issues were found for the compaction path.

Real behavior proof:

Behavior addressed: Telegram message processing failed when OpenClaw appended its own session rows while the embedded prompt lock was released. Two observed rows triggered this class of failure: an auto-compaction row and a later user `message_end` row.

Real environment tested: Private Hugging Face Space `osolmaz/onurclawtest` on `cpu-upgrade`, private bucket volume `osolmaz/onurclawtest-data`, Telegram polling channel enabled.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and pushed `ghcr.io/osolmaz/openclaw-live-test:hf-6563de64`, updated the Space Dockerfile, pushed Space commit `400c2fb0a9791927a0c04b40f49f6c3f849e1213`, then checked Space status and logs with `hf spaces info osolmaz/onurclawtest` and `hf spaces logs osolmaz/onurclawtest --tail 260`.

Evidence after fix: Space reached startup with the fixed image; startup logs showed Telegram DNS/TCP/TLS success, `fetch getMe ok`, gateway ready, model `huggingface/Qwen/Qwen3-8B`, and Telegram polling started.

Observed result after fix: The deployment starts cleanly with the fixed image and the Telegram provider connects. The exact post-rebuild Telegram message proof is still pending.

What was not tested: A new inbound Telegram message after the `hf-6563de64` Space rebuild has not yet been sent/confirmed in this PR body. This PR is draft until that final live message proof is added.

## Risks

The change is narrow, but it touches session locking behavior.
The main risk is accepting too much as owned, so the tests keep the protection boundary explicit.

- The new accepted paths require the existing session write lock and explicit `publishOwnedWrite` option.
- Existing tests still cover rejecting unowned external compaction edits before prompt reacquire.
- External session takeover detection remains active.

## Follow-ups

Add the final post-deploy Telegram message proof before marking ready for review.
